items.name: Developer Program Member
items.how: Join the GitHub Developer Program and build an app with the GitHub API.
items.notes: Shown as a profile badge, not an achievement. The badge depends on active program participation.

items.name: PRO
items.notes: Shown while the account has GitHub Pro.

items.name: Security Bug Bounty Hunter
items.how: Help GitHub find security vulnerabilities through the security program.
items.notes: Not a general-purpose badge; it depends on accepted security work.

items.name: GitHub Campus Expert
items.how: Participate in the GitHub Campus Experts program.
items.notes: Program badge for accepted Campus Experts.

items.name: Security advisory credit
items.how: Submit a security advisory accepted into the GitHub Advisory Database.
items.notes: Shown as a profile badge, not an achievement.

items.name: Arctic Code Vault Contributor
items.how: Contribute to a repository that made the 2020 Arctic Code Vault snapshot.
items.notes: Snapshot date was February 2, 2020. This is historical and no longer earnable.

items.name: Mars 2020 Helicopter Contributor
items.how: Contribute to one of the qualifying repository versions used by NASA Ingenuity.
items.notes: GitHub says the event has ended and the badge is no longer available.

items.name: GitHub Sponsors badge (commonly called Public Sponsor)
items.how: Sponsor a contributor or project through GitHub Sponsors.
items.notes: GitHub's docs/blog describe the badge, while the community often calls it Public Sponsor.

items.name: Galaxy Brain
items.how: Have your reply in GitHub Discussions marked as an accepted / helpful answer.
items.notes: Officially shown in GitHub's launch blog and explained at a high level, but GitHub does not publish all thresholds.

items.name: Pull Shark
items.how: Community consensus: open pull requests that get merged.
items.notes: The name is shown by GitHub in the 2022 changelog image, but detailed criteria and tiers are not officially documented by GitHub.

items.name: YOLO
items.how: Community consensus: merge a pull request without code review.
items.notes: The name is shown by GitHub in the 2022 changelog image, but GitHub does not publish official criteria details.

items.name: Starstruck
items.how: Community-maintained guidance: receive stars on a repository you own.
items.notes: Reported tiers in the community guide: Base 16, Bronze 128, Silver 512, Gold 4096 stars. GitHub does not document this officially.

items.name: Quickdraw
items.how: Community-maintained guidance: close an issue or pull request within 5 minutes of opening it.
items.notes: Commonly described as one-time. GitHub does not document this officially.

items.name: Pair Extraordinaire
items.how: Community-maintained guidance: make co-authored commits on pull requests that get merged.
items.notes: Reported tiers in the community guide: Base 1, Bronze 10, Silver 24, Gold 48. GitHub does not document this officially.

items.name: Heart On Your Sleeve
items.notes: Mentioned in the community guide as listed but not released. No official GitHub criteria were found.

items.name: Open Sourcerer
items.notes: Mentioned in the community guide as listed but not released. No official GitHub criteria were found.
